creator: ohauer@gmx.de
text:
On 2014-03-02 21:44, René Ladan wrote:
> 2014-03-02 21:08 GMT+01:00 olli hauer <ohauer@gmx.de>:
> 
>> Hi,
>>
>> a former colleague asked me some days ago if I have time to look into this
>> port ...
>>
>> Seems Rene was faster, but there are still some issues with the port.
>>
>> I also shaped a patch that rewrites the port to use new port framework
>> parts.
>>
> 
> 
>> - add entry to ports/(G|U)IDs
>>
> I did this, but I probably forgot to add the patch for those files. I
>  chose uid/gid 978, but 187 is fine too of course.
> 
> 
>> - instead using entries twice in pkg-plist
>> %%(AGENT|SERVER)%%/same/file/for/both use
>>   %%SERVER_AGENT%% (similar to NXMAP)
>>
> I was thinking about this, but not sure if that would work/be intended.
> 
> 
>> - move DB backends into a OPTION_GROUP to separate them in the config
>> dialog
>>
> 
> 
>> - sort pkg-plist
>>
> Yeah, mine was more of a rough proposal.
> 
>>
>> Anyway, the ports builds fine with all kind of different options on
>> FreeBSD 8.4,
>> but on 9.2 and on 10 it seems the following files are not build.
>>
>> From `make package' on a 9.2 and 10 system:
>>  pkg-static:
>> lstat(/usr/ports/net-mgmt/netxms/work/stage/usr/local/lib/libnsm_netsvc.so):
>> No such file or directory
>>  pkg-static:
>> lstat(/usr/ports/net-mgmt/netxms/work/stage/usr/local/lib/netxms/netsvc.nsm):
>> No such file or directory
>>
>> The rewritten port can be found here:
>> http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/netxms-1.2.12.diff
>>
>> Unless we have sub-ports, perhaps think about a dedicated netxms-client
>> port (like postgresql$number-(client|server)
>>
>> One last question:
>> Is it really required to `chown netxms:netxms ${DATADIR}' ?
>> This looks not right to me.
>>
>> My patch only changes owner/group of some files inside ${PREFIX}/etc/ ,
> based on the currently committed Makefile.

Hm, yes but pkg-install will try to change the owner of DATADIR during installation ...
I was reading http://wiki.netxms.org/wiki/Server_Installation_Guide and haven't seen
instructions to run chown at all.

> Feel free to commit your version instead.

I would, but I haven't found out why libnsm_netsvc.so and netsvc.nsm is only build
on 8.x and not on 9.x and 10.x (perhaps this is the reason why the port had NO_PACKAGE)

Perhaps the current maintainer can answer the questions about chown, the two files
- usr/local/lib/libnsm_netsvc.so
- lib/netxms/netsvc.nsm

and the NO_PACKAGE before we spend more time to play detective.

-- 
olli
